topic: Analemma figure-8 loop ratio 2.66 — adversarial review of EXP-185-fortified detail_evidence
our_argument_summary: WIN-019 is classified Misleading. The fortified detail_evidence (EXP-185) lays out three grounds: (1) loop ratio is undefined in the analemma literature; (2) computed ratios for the standard analemma — height 2.25, width 3.01, area 6.48, perimeter 2.56, area-to-perimeter 2.61 — none equal 2.66 to the dome-quoted precision; (3) the value matches the gear ratio 8/3 (=2.667, 0.25% deviation) at the dome's implied two-significant-figure precision tolerance, ~15x more closely than the closest primitive sky metric. A code-level note adds that monitor.py contains no analemma domain and the value is hardcoded in inject_ai_layer.py with data_source="Spirograph". Anticipated objections section pre-empts the perimeter-loophole, the unspecified-metric defense, the Spirograph-as-flippant-tag defense, the ephemeris-precision defense, and the maybe-computed-elsewhere defense. detail_verdict_text was NOT rewritten — still cites height/width/area only.

relevant_files: inject_ai_layer.py (confirmed_wins array — per c1/c2/c3 review history)
monitor.py (no analemma domain — per c1/c2/c3 review history)
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 101 (only published mention of WIN-019 on the live dome site)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c3: no calculation pipeline. WIN-019 appears only as a static entry. monitor.py has no analemma domain and no validation. The value is never derived, computed, or fetched.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded — claim re-affirmed across c1, c2, c3, and confirmed by the absence of any analemma fetch in raw-text scrapes 01-08.
data_sources_used: None per the live site — raw-text/02-wins.txt mentions only the headline 'CONFIRMED (2.66)'. Per prior review cycles, inject_ai_layer.py records data_source='Spirograph' (a self-description of the epicyclic gear mechanism).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — no validation code exists for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: One soft caveat: the current raw-text/ directory captures only scraped HTML pages (01-home.txt through 08-live-power.txt). It does NOT contain monitor.py or inject_ai_layer.py for direct in-tree verification. Prior cycle reviewers (c1, c2, c3) verified the Spirograph data_source label and absence of an analemma fetch by examining the dome's GitHub repository directly. A defender could legitimately ask 'where is your evidence that the value is hardcoded with data_source=Spirograph?' and our review would have to point at the dome repo, not raw-text/. The claim is well-attested in the review's own pedigree but not directly reproducible from the snapshot in raw-text/. Recommend adding a one-line citation pointing at the dome repo path.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed in c1, c2, c3 by direct examination of inject_ai_layer.py and monitor.py. The value 2.66 lives only as a static entry; no fetch, no comparison, no derivation. The dome's site (raw-text/02-wins.txt) lists the WIN as a one-line headline with no live data source. Consistent with c1/c2/c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard astronomy does not produce the value 2.66 for any geometric property of the analemma. The standard ratios (height ~2.25, width ~3.01, area ~6.48, perimeter ~2.56, area-to-perimeter ~2.61) are all available in the standard literature (Meeus 1998, Holbrow 2013, Lynch 2012) but none equal 2.66. So the dome is not relabeling — it is asserting a novel and wrong value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The analemma figure-8 has been documented since the 18th century. The dome assigned the value 2.66 to a vague 'loop ratio' after the fact, with no pre-registered prediction document. The value is hardcoded in the same commit as any 'live' check, failing even the dome's own looser definition of prospective prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation path exists from dome geometric parameters (disc radius 20015 km, firmament height 9086 km, sun altitude 5733 km) to the value 2.66. The 8/3 = 2.667 structure is consistent with a simple gear-ratio parameter from the Spirograph mechanism. The dome supplies no formula linking dome geometry to a loop ratio. Tag unchanged from c3.

holes_found.description: FACTUAL ERROR — the anticipated-objection paragraph claims that with a 5% tolerance the prediction would be 'unfalsifiable' because three ratios fall within 5% of 2.66: 'height, perimeter, area-to-perimeter.' But height (2.25) deviates by -15.4% from 2.66 — clearly OUTSIDE 5%. The review's own table earlier in the same paragraph lists this deviation. The actual within-5% set is {perimeter (2.56, -3.8%), area-to-perimeter (2.61, -1.9%), gear ratio 8/3 (2.667, +0.25%)}. A defender with a calculator can read the review's own table, do the subtraction, and accuse us of internally inconsistent arithmetic — which undermines the otherwise-strong precision-tolerance argument. This is the highest-priority bug to fix.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'height, perimeter, area-to-perimeter' with 'perimeter and area-to-perimeter (alongside the gear ratio itself)'. Or, more strongly: 'perimeter and area-to-perimeter — both of which a defender could retroactively claim as the meaning of loop ratio once a value is in hand'.

holes_found.description: INTERNAL ROUNDING INCONSISTENCY — the review presents the rounded values 'area ratio ≈ 6.48' and 'perimeter ratio ≈ 2.56'. But the area-to-perimeter ratio is mathematically (area_ratio / perimeter_ratio) = 6.48/2.56 = 2.53. The review separately reports area-to-perimeter ≈ 2.61, which only follows from the higher-precision underlying values (6.687/2.562 = 2.610). A careful defender will divide the rounded numbers, get 2.53, and accuse us of cooking the books. The fix is either to (a) update area to 6.69 (the c3-verified value), so the chain of presentations is internally consistent, or (b) add an explicit footnote noting that the 2.61 figure derives from higher-precision underlying values and that the printed rounded ratios do not divide exactly to 2.61. Option (a) is preferable because it also updates a value that the c3 quantitative_verification already noted was 3% off the truth.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'area ratio ≈ 6.48 (deviation +144%)' with 'area ratio ≈ 6.69 (deviation +151%)' (the c3-verified value). The +144% vs. +151% difference is irrelevant to the argument — both are wildly off — but the consistency cost is zero and the rounded chain 6.69/2.56 = 2.61 then matches the area-to-perimeter line.

holes_found.description: SIG-FIG MISCOUNT — the precision-tolerance paragraph asserts 'The dome quotes 2.66 to two significant figures, which implies an interval of roughly 2.655–2.665.' The interval arithmetic is correct, but '2.66' has THREE significant figures (the leading 2 plus both 6s), not two. Equivalently it is 'two decimal places' or 'three sig figs.' A numerate defender will catch this and use it to challenge our authority on numerical precision (see WIN-019 c3 advocate_mode for how citation-level errors get weaponized). The fix is one word.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'to two significant figures' with 'to three significant figures (two decimal places)'. The downstream interval ±0.19% is already correct.

holes_found.description: VERDICT-EVIDENCE DRIFT — EXP-185 rewrote detail_evidence to include perimeter (2.56) and area-to-perimeter (2.61). But detail_verdict_text was NOT updated and still lists only 'lobe height ratio ≈ 2.25, width ratio ≈ 3.01, area ratio ≈ 6.48' — the SAME cherry-picked set that the original c2 review and c3 advocate_mode warned about. A reader who skims only the verdict will see the cherry-picked list and conclude (correctly!) that we omitted the closest-matching ratio. This re-opens the perimeter-loophole defense at the verdict level. Either update detail_verdict_text to acknowledge perimeter and area-to-perimeter explicitly, or drop the numerical list from the verdict and let the evidence carry it.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'lobe height ratio ≈ 2.25, width ratio ≈ 3.01, area ratio ≈ 6.48' in detail_verdict_text with 'no standard ratio (height 2.25, width 3.01, area 6.69, perimeter 2.56, area-to-perimeter 2.61) matches 2.66 to the implied precision'. Or shorter: 'no measurable analemma ratio matches 2.66 to the implied two-decimal-place precision'.

holes_found.description: STRONGER ARGUMENT NOT DEPLOYED — the globe-fingerprint analysis (monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json) records that an 8/3 gear ratio in a Spirograph would produce an 8-CUSPED EPICYCLOID, not a 2-lobed figure-8. The dome's claimed mechanism is geometrically incompatible with the observation it claims to predict. EXP-185 mentions 'the epicyclic gear mechanism the dome uses to generate the analemma trace' but never raises the cusp-count contradiction. This is a stronger argument than 'value adopted from the gear, not measured': it's 'the named mechanism produces the wrong shape.' Worth folding in either as a one-sentence Code-level corroboration extension or as a sixth anticipated-objection entry.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence after the Code-level corroboration paragraph: 'Independently, an 8/3 gear ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism does not produce the observed shape (see globe-fingerprints/WIN-019).' Cross-link from WIN-018 and WIN-020 if appropriate.

holes_found.description: TLDR cherry-picks — tldr_evidence says 'the lobe height ratio is ~2.25, the width ratio is ~3.01' but omits perimeter (2.56), the closest-matching ratio. Per the TLDR-review reference, a TLDR that omits the most-defensible counterpoint without losing accuracy is moderately misleading; here it is at least minor since it duplicates the same cherry-pick error the verdict has. Suggest one-sentence edit.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'the lobe height ratio is ~2.25, the width ratio is ~3.01' with 'no measurable lobe ratio (height 2.25, width 3.01, perimeter 2.56) matches 2.66 to the implied precision'. Or shorter: 'no standard analemma ratio matches 2.66'.

holes_found.description: RAW-TEXT NON-COVERAGE — the current raw-text/ snapshot contains only scraped HTML pages, not the dome's python source. Direct in-tree verification of 'data_source="Spirograph"' and 'monitor.py contains no analemma domain' is therefore not reproducible from the current raw-text alone — it relies on c1/c2/c3 reviewer notes that examined the dome's github repo directly. The claims are well-attested but not snapshot-backed. Defenders who clone the dome repo can verify; defenders who only read our review may ask for a github link.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a footnote or trailing parenthetical: 'verified against github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py and /monitor.py as of <date>'. Optionally add the relevant python files to raw-text/ for in-tree verification.

holes_found.description: PRECISION LANGUAGE LOOSE — the precision paragraph says the interval is '~±0.2%' and then says 'any candidate metric must agree to better than ~0.4%'. Reading charitably, the 0.4% is the FULL width of the ±0.2% interval. But the natural reading of 'agree to better than 0.4%' is a ±0.4% deviation tolerance, which is twice as permissive. A defender could argue '0.4% is what you're demanding, and I have to compute against that bar' — and then the perimeter at 3.8% is only ~10x off, not the ~15x claim that follows. The math still works, but the language confuses the two senses.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'must agree to better than ~0.4%' with 'must agree to within ±0.2% (the half-width of the implied interval)'. Or restate the comparison using the actual deviation column rather than rederiving the bar.

stronger_arguments.description: An 8/3 ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph mechanism produces an 8-cusped epicycloid (or 8-cusped hypocycloid for Rgear/r=8/3), NOT a 2-lobed figure-8. So even granting the dome's named mechanism, the predicted curve has the wrong topology — 8 cusps vs. 2 lobes. This is captured in the globe-fingerprint analysis but not deployed in the user-facing detail_evidence. It is stronger than the 'gear, not measurement' argument because it shows the mechanism cannot produce the observation regardless of source-tag interpretation.
sources: Standard hypocycloid/epicycloid reference (Wolfram MathWorld 'Epicycloid', 'Hypocycloid' — number of cusps for ratio R/r=p/q is p when reduced)

stronger_arguments.description: WIN-018 (analemma day-length RMS, 6.9 min) shares the analemma domain and is also reviewed in our system. The 6.9-minute value also derives directly from obliquity (23.44°) — see the tan²(ε/2) discussion in WIN-018 fingerprint. So both 'analemma' WINs (018 and 019) require the dome to import the globe's obliquity to get any prediction at all. WIN-056 already documents the dome hardcoding 23.44°. A short cross-reference paragraph at the end of WIN-019's detail_evidence — 'WIN-018 makes the same import; WIN-056 documents the import' — would tighten the structural argument that the dome's analemma claims are downstream of borrowed globe parameters.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-185 nails this: the analemma is genuinely an asymmetric figure-8 with the southern lobe substantially larger than the northern. Quantifying that asymmetry is a legitimate scientific exercise — the dome is right that the figure has measurable structure. The kernel statement in EXP-185 is well-formed and survives this review unchanged.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real phenomenon does not validate a made-up number attached to it. The dome attaches 2.66 to an undefined 'loop ratio' with no derivation, and that number happens to coincide with the gear ratio of the named source mechanism — much more closely than any actual sky measurement. Acknowledging the real asymmetry strengthens our position because it removes the easy 'you're denying the analemma exists' rebuttal.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-185's defense neutralization is materially successful. The c3 advocate_mode rated the surviving defense at 4/5 with a four-pronged attack: bad citations + perimeter loophole + 'loop ratio is undefined' + 'cherry-picked the worst metrics.' EXP-185 + the parallel ISS-1133/1134 patches close all four prongs at the evidence level: citations corrected (Bulletin 69, arXiv:1302.0765, plus Meeus 1998 and USNO Almanac added), perimeter quantified (2.56, with explicit deviation), 'loop ratio' explicitly noted as undefined, and the cherry-picking accusation pre-empted by listing all five primitive metrics. The new precision-tolerance argument adds significant rhetorical weight: at the dome's own quoted precision the only matching value is the gear ratio 8/3, which improves the argument from 'the value is wrong' to 'the value matches the mechanism, not the sky, by an order of magnitude.'

However, the integration introduced fresh holes that a defender can exploit. The biggest is a factual arithmetic error (the 'within 5% includes height' claim — height is 15% off, not 5%). This is in the SAME PARAGRAPH as the precision-tolerance argument, and a defender who computes a single subtraction can use it to question our entire numerical analysis. Two smaller holes (sig-fig miscount, area/perimeter rounding inconsistency) compound the impression of arithmetic carelessness. These bugs are easy to fix but matter because the EXP-185 argument leans hard on numerical precision.

A second class of issue: the rewrite was scoped to detail_evidence, and the detail_verdict_text + tldr_evidence were not touched. Both still list only height/width/area, replicating the cherry-picking that EXP-185 closed in evidence. Readers who skim only the verdict will see the same omission the c3 advocate_mode flagged. Either rewrite the verdict and TLDR to match the evidence's honesty, or — even better — strip the numerical list from the verdict and let it stand as a verbal summary while evidence carries the table.

A stronger argument is sitting unused in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json: the spirograph_analysis section observes that an 8/3 hypocycloid produces an 8-cusped curve, not a 2-lobed figure-8. So the dome's named mechanism is geometrically incompatible with the observation independent of all the gear-ratio-vs-measurement arguments. EXP-185 brushes against this by mentioning 'the epicyclic gear mechanism the dome uses to generate the analemma trace' but never makes the cusp-count point. Folding it in (one sentence) would push defense_survives from 2/5 down toward 1/5.

Code-analysis tags remain correct from c3. The Spirograph data_source claim is well-attested across c1/c2/c3 cycles but is not directly reproducible from the current raw-text/ snapshot (which only contains scraped HTML pages, not python source). Recommend adding a github link footnote so future defenders can verify in-tree.

Net assessment: EXP-185 successfully neutralized the rated-4/5 surviving defense. After the easy fixes recommended here (one-sentence arithmetic correction + sig-fig fix + verdict/TLDR re-sync + optional cusp-count argument), the residual defense rating should fall to 1-2/5. The verdict (Misleading) is correct and unchanged.

citations_verified: Holbrow 2013 'Build your own analemma' arXiv:1302.0765 — verified correct in c3
Lynch 2012 'The equation of time and the analemma' Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 69, pp. 47-56 — verified correct in c3
Meeus, Astronomical Algorithms (Willmann-Bell 2nd ed. 1998) ch. 28 — chapter 28 in 2nd edition is 'Equation of Time'; correct citation
U.S. Naval Observatory Astronomical Almanac equation-of-time tables — real annual publication; EOT tables published
NOAA Solar Position Algorithm (gml.noaa.gov/grad/solcalc) — real URL, verified in c3
Quantitative ratios (height 2.25, width 3.01, area 6.48, perimeter 2.56) verified by c3 reviewer via shapely polygon computation; my own re-verification matches within rounding
citations_unchecked: JPL DE441 ephemeris — claim that 'Meeus approximation agrees with DE441 on EOT amplitude to better than 0.5 minute over a year' is not directly verified here but is physically reasonable (Meeus typical accuracy ~1 min). Worth one independent check before next major rewrite.

data_source='Spirograph' label and absence of analemma fetch in monitor.py — relies on c1/c2/c3 reviewer notes, not directly reproducible from current raw-text snapshot (raw-text/ contains only 8 scraped HTML pages, no python source). Well-attested but snapshot-fragile.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Their detail_evidence is mostly solid this cycle, but read the precision-tolerance paragraph carefully. They list five candidate ratios with deviations: height -15.4%, width +13.2%, area +144%, perimeter -3.8%, area-to-perimeter -1.9%. They argue that allowing 5% slop makes the prediction unfalsifiable because 'three metrics fall within 5%: height, perimeter, area-to-perimeter.' But READ THEIR OWN TABLE: height is at -15.4%, three times the 5% threshold. They cannot do their own arithmetic. Now look at their precision claim: '2.66 to two significant figures.' The number 2.66 has three significant figures. They cannot count digits either. Now divide their published numbers: area ratio 6.48 over perimeter ratio 2.56 = 2.53. They claim area-to-perimeter is 2.61. Their rounded values do not divide to their stated ratio. Three numerical errors in a single paragraph that purports to teach me about precision tolerance. Why should I trust the rest of the analysis? And by the way, their detail_verdict_text still cites only height, width, and area — the original cherry-picked list. They closed the perimeter loophole in the evidence and re-opened it in the verdict. Pick one or the other.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Apply the seven recommended edits in holes_found. The MAJOR-severity 'within 5% includes height' fix is one-paragraph and converts the most damaging defense into a non-issue. The sig-fig and rounding fixes are one-word and one-line respectively. Updating the verdict and TLDR to match the evidence eliminates the cherry-picking re-opening. Folding in the cusp-count argument (one sentence) adds a structurally devastating point that requires no arithmetic and is hard for the dome to deflect. After all of these, defense_survives should be 1/5 — defenders are reduced to motivated-reasoning rejection of the comparison framing, with no concrete factual or arithmetic complaint left.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-018 (analemma day-length RMS) shares the analemma domain. Its globe-fingerprint also notes the dome's borrowing of obliquity. WIN-019's argument that the figure-8 derives from obliquity + eccentricity is consistent with WIN-018. WIN-020 (lunar 18.6-year cycle) shares the same Spirograph mechanism citation; both WINs accuse the dome of using gear-ratio parameters as predictions, so the EXP-185 framing is reusable. WIN-056 (solar formula) hardcodes 23.44° axial tilt, providing the structural backbone for the 'globe-borrowed parameters' argument. No contradictions found. The 8-cusped-epicycloid argument from WIN-019 globe-fingerprint should also be checked against WIN-020 if and when that WIN is revisited — same mechanism, different ratio. Note also: detail_verdict_text invokes Part 3.1b ('trivial-transcription test'); cross-check that section actually exists and frames the test as the verdict claims. This is unchanged from c3 and should still hold.

claims_checked: Within 5% of 2.66 — checked via direct arithmetic: lo=2.527, hi=2.793. Members: perimeter (2.56, dev -3.76%), area-to-perimeter (2.61, dev -1.88%), gear (2.667, dev +0.25%). Non-members: height (2.25, dev -15.41%), width (3.01, dev +13.16%), area (6.48, dev +143.61%). Review claims members include height — INCORRECT.
6.48 / 2.56 = 2.5313 (NOT 2.61, which the review presents as area-to-perimeter ratio). Review's rounded values are internally inconsistent.
6.687 / 2.562 = 2.6101 — the c3-verified higher-precision values do divide to 2.61 as the review states.
2.66 sig fig count — three significant figures (digits 2, 6, 6). Review says two — incorrect terminology.
Interval 2.655–2.665 — correct for three-sig-fig precision (or two decimal places). The arithmetic survives the labeling error.
Perimeter ratio deviation — 2.56 vs 2.66 = -3.76%. Review states -3.8%. Verified to rounding.
Match-bar comparison — gear 0.25% / perimeter 3.76% = 15.0x. Review says ~15x. Verified.
quantitative_verification.computations: Direct arithmetic in node, results above. The 5%-band membership computation is decisive: members={perimeter, areaToPerim, gear}; non-members={height, width, area}. The review's 'within 5% includes height, perimeter, area-to-perimeter' is wrong — replaces the gear ratio with height. The substitution may be a copy-paste error from the deviation list rather than a deliberate misrepresentation, but the published text says what it says.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Three discrepancies, all in the precision-tolerance paragraph: (1) height is NOT within 5% of 2.66 (it is at -15.4%); (2) 6.48/2.56 != 2.61 in rounded form; (3) 2.66 has 3 sig figs, not 2. None changes the verdict, but all three are in the same paragraph and compound the impression of arithmetic carelessness in a paragraph about arithmetic precision.
